heading: Use of local currency payment

L. 86–500, title V, § 50974 Stat. 186Pub. L. 110–246, title III, § 3001(c)122 Stat. 1821June 8, 1960June 18, 2008“Notwithstanding the provisions of any other law, at least 75 per centum of the total cost of any family housing project or community facility hereafter constructed or acquired in any foreign country by the Department of Defense or any military department shall be paid for from foreign currencies acquired by the Commodity Credit Corporation pursuant to the provisions of the Food for Peace Act [this chapter]; except that the following projects authorized pursuant to this Act shall not be subject to this requirement:“Air Force activities, Japan, one hundred fifty units;“Cigli Air Base, Turkey, community facilities;“Clark Air Force Base, Philippine Islands, one hundred units;“Iraklion Air Station, Crete, community facilities;“Kirknewton RAF, United Kingdom, fifty units;“Naval Air Station, Naha, Okinawa, two hundred; and“Site I–5, Italy, one hundred units and community facilities.”

Payment From Foreign Currencies for Family Housing Projects or Community Facilities Constructed or Acquired by Department of Defense, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 86–500, title V, § 50974 Stat. 186Pub. L. 110–246, title III, § 3001(c)122 Stat. 1821June 8, 1960June 18, 2008“Notwithstanding the provisions of any other law, at least 75 per centum of the total cost of any family housing project or community facility hereafter constructed or acquired in any foreign country by the Department of Defense or any military department shall be paid for from foreign currencies acquired by the Commodity Credit Corporation pursuant to the provisions of the Food for Peace Act [this chapter]; except that the following projects authorized pursuant to this Act shall not be subject to this requirement:“Air Force activities, Japan, one hundred fifty units;“Cigli Air Base, Turkey, community facilities;“Clark Air Force Base, Philippine Islands, one hundred units;“Iraklion Air Station, Crete, community facilities;“Kirknewton RAF, United Kingdom, fifty units;“Naval Air Station, Naha, Okinawa, two hundred; and“Site I–5, Italy, one hundred units and community facilities.”

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Agreements under this subchapter may provide that the Secretary shall use payments made in local currencies by the developing country or private entity in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Special account
children.label: b Special account
children.text: Foreign currencies received by the Secretary under this subchapter shall be deposited in a separate account, that may be interest-bearing, to the credit of the United States and such currencies and interest thereon shall be used as provided for in this section.

children.label: A
children.text: increased agricultural production, including availability of agricultural inputs, with emphasis on small farms, processing of agricultural commodities, forestry management, and land and water management;

children.label: B
children.text: credit policies for private-sector agriculture development;

children.label: C
children.text: establishment and expansion of institutions for basic and applied agricultural research and the use of such research through development of extension services;

children.label: D
children.text: programs to control rodents, insects, weeds, and other animal or plant pests; and

children.label: E
children.text: the improvement of the trade capacity of the recipient country.

children.heading: Agricultural business development loans
children.label: 2 Agricultural business development loans
children.text: To make loans to United States business entities (including cooperatives) and branches, subsidiaries, or affiliates of such entities for development of agricultural businesses and agricultural trade capacity in such appropriate developing countries.

children.heading: Agricultural facilities loans
children.label: 3 Agricultural facilities loans
children.text: To make loans to domestic or foreign entities (including cooperatives) for the establishment of facilities for aiding in the utilization or distribution of agricultural products.

children.heading: Trade promotion
children.label: 4 Trade promotion
children.text: To promote agricultural trade development, under procedures established by the Secretary, by making loans or through other activities (including trade fairs to promote agricultural products produced in appropriate developing countries) that the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Private sector agricultural trade development
children.label: 5 Private sector agricultural trade development
children.text: To conduct private sector agricultural trade development activities in the appropriate developing country, as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Research
children.label: 6 Research
children.text: To conduct research in agriculture, forestry, and aquaculture, including collaborative research which is mutually beneficial to the United States and the appropriate developing country.

children.heading: United States obligations
children.label: 7 United States obligations
children.text: To make payments of United States obligations (including obligations entered into pursuant to other laws).

children.heading: Safe water and sanitation
children.label: 8 Safe water and sanitation
children.text: To provide assistance under section 2152h of title 22 to promote good health, economic development, poverty reduction, women’s empowerment, conflict prevention, and environmental sustainability by increasing affordable and equitable access to safe water and sanitation.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Fiscal requirements regarding use of local currencies
children.label: d Fiscal requirements regarding use of local currencies

children.heading: Exemption
children.label: 1 Exemption
children.text: shall not apply to local currencies used by the President under paragraphs (1) through (7) of subsection (c).Section 1306 of title 31

children.heading: Use of currencies by other agencies
children.label: 2 Use of currencies by other agencies
children.text: Any department or agency of the Federal Government other than the Department of Agriculture using any such local currencies for a purpose for which funds have been appropriated shall reimburse the Commodity Credit Corporation in an amount equivalent to the dollar value of the currencies used.
